Discussion 'Mac Lion (<hide>.<hide>)' started axboi<hide>, Jan <hide>, <hide>.
I've got <hide>gb internal drive and <hide>gb SSD.
When trying restore using disk utility i'm given the error "Not enough space disk ____ restore"
But shouldn't have that!!!
Any ideas workarounds before resorting the above?
Use Carbon Copy Cloner copy one drive the other.
I've done this several times going from larger HDD smaller SSD and wound with bootable SSD drive.
One step you have remember not skip use Disk Utility partition the SSD GUID partition scheme HFS+ before doing the clone.
came Apple Partition Scheme, even you let CCC the clone, the resulting drive won't bootable.
CCC usually works "file mode" and can easily copy larger drive (that's mostly empty) onto smaller drive.
you tell CCC clone drive you did NOT boot from, can work block copy mode where the destination drive must the same size larger than the drive you are cloning from (if recall).
I've actually done this somehow Disk Utility several times (booting from different drive (or even the dvd) not running disk utility from the drive your cloning) and had work just fine from larger smaller bootable clone.
Definitely format the drive cloning first, bootable Apple etc..
Thank

for pointing this out.
only experience using larger smaller was when was trying make Lion install stick and was unable restore InstallESD.dmg USB stick but course the reason that wouldn't fit there was slightly more than data.

Sysco Corp. has terminated its planned $<hide>.<hide> billion takeover Foods, announced Monday, after federal judge blocked the combination.
The company opting instead add billion its stock-buyback program.
With the deal breaking up, Sysco will pay $<hide> million termination fee Foods and $<hide>.<hide> million fee Performance Food Group, which had agreed buy some Foods facilities.
Sysco, based Houston, plans make the share repurchases over the next two years.
Rosemont, Illinois-based Foods operates major distribution center Fishers.
Sysco has large warehouse <hide> <hide>nd St., Indianapolis.
Sysco had fought for more than year gain government approval for the transaction, which antitrust regulators said would hurt competition and lead higher prices.
Sysco and Foods dominate market known broadline foodservice, which supplies school cafeterias, restaurants and hotels.
Sysco had argued that the acquisition would bring billion savings, letting offer lower prices customers.
Investors have responded with relief the deal’s demise, reflecting concerns about the company undertaking ambitious merger.
Sysco shares rose <hide>.<hide> percent the day the transaction was halted U.S. District Judge Amit Mehta, and the stock climbe

again Monday morning after the merger was withdrawn.
Mehta blocked the merger June when granted Federal Trade Commission request delay the transaction.
The FTC had sued the companies February, saying the deal would give Sysco oversized share industry where it’s already the biggest player.
arguments before Mehta May, the two sides clashed over the scope the market which the companies compete.
Sysco and Foods argued that the commission was relying “tortured” analysis, ignoring the variety distribution channels available customers.
Sysco said Monday that weighed embarking appeal but decided against it.
Shares Sysco rose much <hide>.<hide> percent $<hide> early trading.
The stock had slid <hide>.<hide> percent this year through the end last week.
